user.description: The user to run the environment as.

install.description: The update script to run on VM startup (after pulling latest changes) to refresh dependencies.

start.description: The start command to run when the environment is started.

repositoryDependencies.description: Repositories that are required for the environment to work, and need to be included in the GitHub access token that is generated for the environment.

items.description: The URL of the dependent repository, e.g. `github.com/org/repo`.

ports.description: Ports to expose from the container. Similar to devcontainers port forwarding.

name.description: A descriptive name for the port (e.g., 'web server', 'api').

port.description: The port number inside the container to expose.

terminals.description: The terminals to run when the environment is started.

command.description: The command to run in the terminal.

description.description: A description of what the terminal does. This is displayed to the agent.

command.description: The command to run in the terminal.

description.description: A description of what the terminal does. This is displayed to the agent.

dockerfile.description: The location of the Dockerfile that defines the contents of the container. The path is relative to the folder containing the `environment.json` file.

context.description: The location of the context folder for building the Docker image. The path is relative to the folder containing the `environment.json` file.

snapshot.description: A snapshot ID for the base environment.

agentCanUpdateSnapshot.description: Whether the agent can update the snapshot.
